If you find a group of coworkers that share and appreciate such behavior, you will find your work life much more enjoyable 😉
Happy New Year to All! 🧨🤪

author: @charleselertii6187
text:
Wow Robert! The pack of cigarettes rolled up in your white T-shirt sleeve reminded me of my Dad in 1963. Even though it was a passing fad from the early 1950s. Fabulous. Of course, he didn't have the rough "lower working class" Brit accent. (We were born and raised factory workers in Detroit Michigan)
My Dad was born in 1930. He dressed like that when he was wearing his "Dungarees". But back in his single days before he married my Mom in 1955, when he and his peers showed up for a date, they all wore dress pants, dress shirt, and skinny ties...
They hoped to catch the ladies to ask them for a date wearing the "hoodlum look garb" and having a clean car.
Then dress to impress the ladies and the parents when they arrived to pick up their Saturday night date.
Chuck in Florida.
